triggerSignal: Churn risk flag: 'not sure whether they'll renew at this price point'
rationale: Two consecutive meetings with negative sentiment and churn language. Renewal in 30 days. Escalation to executive level is required to understand root cause and retention path before renewal window closes.

draftContent.subject: Checking in — want to make sure we're set up for success
draftContent.body:
Hi Marcus,

I wanted to reach out personally. I know your team has had some challenges getting full value from the platform, and with renewal coming up, I'd love to get on a call to understand what would make this a clear win for CloudKitchen Pro.

Would you have 30 minutes this week or next?

Best,
[CSM Name]

triggerSignal: Active user rate dropped to 23% (8 of 35 users). Only 2 of 8 modules actively used.
rationale: Product adoption is the root cause of the ROI dissatisfaction. A targeted guided onboarding for kitchen staff could recover adoption without requiring feature changes. Modules unused: Inventory Forecasting, Staff Scheduling, Performance Analytics, Analytics Exports, Benchmark Reporting, API Reports.

draftContent.subject: Quick adoption plan for your kitchen team
draftContent.body:
Hi Marcus,

I've put together a focused 2-week plan to get your kitchen staff up and running on the core modules. It's lightweight — designed for teams that don't have time for training sessions.

Week 1: Inventory Forecasting (video walkthrough + 30-min live Q&A)
Week 2: Staff Scheduling (quick-start template + async Slack support)

This covers the two modules your team would benefit from most immediately. Would you be open to trying this approach?

triggerSignal: Renewal in 30 days + health score < 50 + two negative meetings
rationale: High-risk renewal with adoption below threshold. A time-limited incentive tied to adoption milestones can bridge the renewal gap while the team recovers usage. Recommend offering 60-day extended access to two modules as a retention bridge.
note: Internal action — creates a task for CSM to develop retention offer proposal. Does not contact customer.

triggerSignal: API performance issue open for 3 weeks — 'their IT team has been complaining for weeks about the API response times and nobody has resolved the ticket'
rationale: Unresolved technical issue contributing to customer dissatisfaction. Support ticket has been open 3 weeks with no resolution. Escalation to engineering is required before renewal conversation.

draftContent.subject: Escalation request — FranchiseWorks API performance
draftContent.body:
[Internal — Engineering Team]

Escalating the FranchiseWorks API performance issue as Priority-1. This account renews in 65 days and the open ticket is cited as a key satisfaction driver. Customer has explicitly mentioned it in last 2 meetings.

Ticket: [ticket ID]
Open since: [date]
Customer impact: Regional franchise rollout stalled

Need response and resolution plan within 48 hours.

triggerSignal: Expansion signal: 'regional VP is interested in rolling this out to 3 more hospital sites' + 'finance team is planning to include a budget line in Q1'
rationale: Expansion opportunity identified with Q1 budget cycle alignment. CSM should prepare and deliver a proposal before the Q1 budget cycle closes (target: deliver by November 2026). Account is healthy — expansion is the primary opportunity here.

draftContent.subject: Proposal for regional expansion — 3 additional sites
draftContent.body:
Hi Kavya,

Thank you for the kind words in our last call — it's great to hear the clinical team is seeing real results. I've been thinking about the regional expansion conversation and want to make sure we have a proposal ready before your Q1 budget cycle.

I'll put together a tailored proposal for the 3-site expansion, including implementation timeline and pricing. Can I get 30 minutes with you and Anand to walk through it?

Best,
[CSM Name]
